title: chenopodium ambrosioides f. suffruticosum
intro: Chenopodium ambrosioides f. suffruticosum, also known as Mexican tea or epazote, is a subspecies of the Chenopodium ambrosioides plant, a type of flowering plant in the amaranth family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a natural digestive aid, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial agent. The health benefits of Mexican tea include the relief of symptoms associated with indigestion, nausea, and diarrhea, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve heart health. The plant can be prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, with the tea being the most commonly used herbal preparation, often consumed in small amounts to reap its medicinal benefits.
